entry.title: Combined summary - Regular signet reorgs
entry.summary: The recent advancements in blockchain technology have seen the implementation of a novel method for inducing regular, automatic blockchain reorganizations (reorgs) on the signet network. This process is programmed to occur daily, with the exact timing determined by a cryptographically random trigger. The technical procedure involves a bash script that retrieves the current best block hash and applies a SHA-256 hash function combined with a secret value. If the output, when taken modulo 144, equals zero, it triggers a reorganization by invalidating a specified block. A noteworthy example of this mechanism's effectiveness was observed at [block 307073](https://mempool.space/signet/block/00000006e627e02fe27eec8dbf1fc2a83839c8d92475ba807c99d3a118f035b4), which underwent a two-block reorganization. This incident not only demonstrates the operational capabilities of the strategy but also raises important questions about the expected duration and frequency of such reorganizations. In addition to the technical details of the reorg strategy, the updates on the fork-observer instance have reinstated and enhanced accessibility to the signet network, observable through [this link](https://fork.observer/?network=3). During recent observations, a two-block reorganization at block height 307073 was noted, where the system effectively identified the coinbase tags linked to different miners. Notably, the tag 'signet-3 (inquisition)' was associated with one set of mining activities, while 'signet-1' was tied to an individual named Kalle. This kind of tagging and tracking is pivotal in analyzing the contributions and dynamics within the network, offering insights into the active participants and their roles in the blockchain operations. Additionally, there exists a fallback mechanism labeled 'signet-2', poised to take over block building should the 'inquisition' node encounter issues, further illustrating the robustness and intricate planning embedded within the network's operational framework. These developments provide a fertile ground for both theoretical exploration and practical application in the realm of blockchain technology, particularly in understanding and predicting behaviors under modified operational conditions. The use of hardcoded constants like `$SECRET` in the hashing process introduces additional computational challenges and opportunities for speculation, enriching the overall scope of research and analysis in this innovative field.
